steps.title: Estimate the slope
steps.content: The dots rise by about $3$ feet in long-jump distance for each $1$ foot increase in high-jump height. So the line of best fit should have a slope near $3$, not a slope near $0.82$.

steps.title: Estimate the intercept
steps.content: The trend line would cross the $y$-axis a little above $0$, around $0.8$. A negative intercept such as $-3.30$ would put the line too low for the data.

steps.title: Match the model
steps.content: The equation $$ y=3.30x+0.82 $$ has the right steep positive slope and a reasonable positive intercept. The answer is C.
